characters.name: Elias Voss
characters.description: The owner of the estate, composed and unreadable.

thrillerProfile.innocentRead: Elias is protecting an old family scandal that predates Mara.
thrillerProfile.dangerousRead: Elias staged the invitation and swapped Mara into a role meant for a missing guest.

characters.name: Sabine Vale
characters.description: The estate's housekeeper, territorial, efficient, and impossible to rush.

thrillerProfile.innocentRead: Sabine cleans up after the house and protects it from scandal.
thrillerProfile.dangerousRead: Sabine clears names, trays, and evidence before Elias can be caught.

characters.name: Clara Vale
characters.description: The missing guest whose name has been removed from the gallery and visitor tray.

thrillerProfile.innocentRead: Clara fled when she realized the evening was staged against her.
thrillerProfile.dangerousRead: Clara engineered the substitution and vanished to weaponize Mara's arrival.

locations.name: Foyer
locations.description: A cold marble foyer with a locked side door.

locations.name: Hallway
locations.description: A narrow hall lined with family portraits.

locations.name: Service Landing
locations.description: A narrow landing where staff trays, visitor cards, and household linen pass out of sight.

clues.label: Storm cut the road
clues.question: Why does Elias stress that Mara cannot leave?
clues.summary: Elias frames the visit as unavoidable as soon as Mara arrives.

clues.label: Fresh scratches on the side door
clues.question: Who used the sealed side door recently?
clues.summary: The lock was disturbed after the estate was supposed to be closed.

clues.label: Scratched brass around the lock
clues.question: Was the hidden room accessed in haste?
clues.summary: The metal around the latch is newly scored.

clues.label: Hidden key under the brass bowl
clues.question: Why keep the key close if the room is forbidden?
clues.summary: The key is concealed in reach, not archived.

clues.label: Warm brass bowl
clues.question: Who touched the foyer console moments ago?
clues.summary: The bowl is still rocking when Mara reaches it.

clues.label: Elias watches the side door
clues.question: Why does Elias monitor that threshold so closely?
clues.summary: His attention returns to the forbidden door before Mara names it.

clues.label: Elias flinched at the hidden room
clues.question: What does the room represent to him?
clues.summary: Mara can provoke a real reaction by naming what should be off limits.

clues.label: Elias controls the route through the house
clues.question: What is he trying to keep outside Mara's line of sight?
clues.summary: He guides Mara without touching her, always one room ahead.

clues.label: Burned film smell returns in the hall
clues.question: What piece of old media was heated or destroyed tonight?
clues.summary: The chemical smell from the foyer grows stronger near the portraits.

clues.label: Elias rehearsed the welcome
clues.question: Was tonight staged for a different kind of guest?
clues.summary: His hospitality sounds practiced rather than spontaneous.

clues.label: Portrait eyes are scratched away
clues.question: Which witness was erased from the family record?
clues.summary: The damage is deliberate and selective.

clues.label: One portrait nameplate is missing
clues.question: Which absent name mattered enough to remove?
clues.summary: A brass mount remains, but the identifying plate is gone.

clues.label: Projector inventory gap
clues.question: Which projection component was removed from the house?
clues.summary: Soot, dust, and a clean rectangular gap imply a missing case or reel.

clues.label: Guest card removed from the gallery ledger
clues.question: Who was expected before Mara arrived?
clues.summary: The empty slot in the visitor ledger turns the evening into a substitution.

clues.label: Elias knows the hidden room matters
clues.question: Why does he answer accusation with craft instead of surprise?
clues.summary: Elias treats the room as a script problem, not a storage problem.

clues.label: Sabine has direct access to the gallery and visitor tray
clues.question: Could a staff hand have removed the card and nameplate?
clues.summary: The service route lets Sabine reach the tray and portraits without crossing the front hall.

clues.label: Sabine protects the house before the truth
clues.question: Is Sabine hiding scandal or causing it?
clues.summary: Her first instinct is to defend the estate's surface rather than answer Mara's questions.

clues.label: The missing guest left a warning note
clues.question: Did the missing guest know the evening had been staged?
clues.summary: A folded note suggests someone expected a second performance and wanted the next witness to flee.

clues.label: The missing guest knew the house schedule
clues.question: Was the missing guest inside the plan or outside it?
clues.summary: The warning note assumes knowledge of the reel change and staff route timing.

thriller.endingContract: Caution without leverage leaves Mara outside the truth and outside the room built to contain it.

body.text: Mara tests the handle once, then again. The lock does not move, and Elias says nothing that sounds like surprise.

thriller.endingContract: Pressure applied too early wins an admission, but not access.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: If you accuse me while the gallery is still doing the accusing for you, Mara, all you learn is how practiced I am at surviving the first question.

thriller.endingContract: Restraint preserves the relationship surface long enough for Mara to leave with a causal theory instead of a confession.

body.text: Mara says nothing. Elias mistakes her silence for patience, and that mistake buys her the sequence he was trying to protect: a missing nameplate, an empty guest-card slot, and a projection case removed before she arrived.
She leaves without a confession, but not without a theory. Someone else was meant to stand where she stands now, and Elias rebuilt the evening fast enough to swap the witness but not the evidence.

thriller.endingContract: Naming the sequence forces Elias to admit the invitation was staged for someone else before Mara took the slot.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: You were not supposed to see the wall before the reel. That was the order of it.

The answer is not a confession so much as a correction. Elias had prepared a projection, a room, and a role for a guest who never arrived. Mara is only dangerous because she noticed the substitution before the performance began.

thriller.endingContract: Pressing Sabine makes the housekeeper theory feel brutally plausible, even if the full architecture still points beyond her.

body.speaker: char.housekeeper
body.text: I remove what keeps this house standing. Names, cards, broken glass, gossip. If that makes me your villain, at least choose a useful one.

Sabine never admits to staging the invitation, but she does admit she will erase almost anything to keep the estate from collapsing in public. It is enough to leave Mara with a second suspect theory that does not depend on Elias alone.

thriller.endingContract: Following the note keeps the missing guest theory alive and reframes the evening as a trap with an absent author.

body.text: The note is too specific to be a random warning. Whoever wrote it knew the reel might be changed, knew there would be a second performance, and knew another witness could be slotted in after the first guest vanished.
Mara leaves the house with a third theory still burning: the missing guest did not merely disappear from Elias's script. She may have rewritten it and left Mara to read the new draft alone.

nodes.title: Start
nodes.notes:
# Start

Runtime entry node. No prose lives here.

nodes.title: Foyer Arrival

thriller.promise: The house may already know why Mara came, and Elias is controlling what she sees first.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: Why is the side door recently used if the estate is supposed to be sealed for the storm?

body.text: The foyer smells of rain, old varnish, and something hotter underneath, like film left too close to a lamp.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: Welcome. The storm cut the road behind you ten minutes ago, so whatever brought you here is staying the night.

choices.text: Test the scratched side door before Elias can redirect you.

risks: Elias sees that Mara is already looking for a hidden room.

choices.text: Let Elias choose the route and study what he avoids mentioning.

risks: The player gives up immediate access to the side door.

nodes.notes:
# Foyer Arrival

Use `scene` for most visual novel moments.

nodes.title: Door Check

thriller.promise: The side door may hide the real reason Elias invited Mara before the road closed.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: If the door is supposed to stay locked, why are the scratches fresh and the brass bowl warm?

body.text: Fresh brass dust glitters around the lock. A shallow bowl on the console table still rocks from being moved in a hurry.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: That room is full of old equipment. Nothing in there is meant to survive a curious guest.

choices.text: Lift the bowl and try the hidden key before Elias can stop you.

risks: Elias now knows Mara will cross a boundary to get an answer.

choices.text: Leave the lock alone and watch whether Elias flinches when you name the hidden room.

risks: Elias regains control of the route before Mara gains access.

choices.text: Step back before Elias can catch you crossing the line.

risks: The hidden room closes again before Mara gains leverage.

nodes.notes:
# Door Check

Investigation pressure scene with a visible access tradeoff.

nodes.title: Hallway Lead

thriller.promise: Elias has won the first exchange and is trying to turn that advantage into narrative control.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: What did Elias need Mara to miss while he kept her moving?

body.text: Elias keeps half a step ahead of Mara, as if the hall itself had taught him how to guide a guest away from the wrong door without ever touching her.
The hot, chemical smell from the foyer returns between the portraits, stronger now that she knows to look for it.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: A house is easiest to misread when the host looks cooperative.

choices.text: Keep following and look for what the hallway records instead of what Elias says.

risks: Elias keeps the initiative while Mara studies the environment.

nodes.notes:
# Hallway Lead

Route-specific aftermath of letting Elias set the pace.

nodes.title: Hallway Recovery

thriller.promise: Mara has rattled Elias, but not enough to stop him from reclaiming control of the house.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: Why did Elias react to the hidden room as if Mara had spoken a forbidden name?

body.text: Elias flinches once, almost invisibly, then turns the motion into an invitation deeper into the hall.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: You came here expecting a confession. Most visitors only expect hospitality.

choices.text: Let him recover his composure and study what the corridor preserved from the slip.

risks: Elias restores his mask before Mara can force a second mistake.

nodes.notes:
# Hallway Recovery

Route-specific aftermath of confronting Elias at the door.

nodes.title: Portrait Gallery

thriller.promise: The gallery stops behaving like mood and starts behaving like a ledger.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: Did Elias erase one witness from the gallery so Mara would mistake a substitution for an invitation?

body.text: By the time they reach the portrait gallery, Elias has the lead again, but the house has stopped helping him. Every canvas has the eyes scored away, and one brass mount gleams bare where a nameplate was removed so recently the dust has not settled back in.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: Families remove faces when they cannot bear what those faces witnessed. Names are harder to survive than faces.

choices.text: Accuse Elias now, before he can recover the missing name into another polite lie.

risks: Pushing before the gallery yields method or timeline evidence may force Elias into pure performance.

choices.text: Let Elias mistake your silence for hesitation and read the empty mount instead of his face.

risks: Elias gets one more chance to perform innocence while Mara studies the evidence.

nodes.notes:
# Portrait Gallery

Shared pressure scene after the route-specific hallway lead-in.

nodes.title: Nameplate Audit

thriller.promise: The missing plate, visitor ledger, and projector residue turn atmosphere into method.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: Was Mara invited for herself, or slotted into a role prepared for someone who never arrived?

theorySeeds.basis: Elias staged the substitution and removed the visible traces too late.
A staff hand could have cleared the tray and plate while protecting the house.
The missing guest may have stripped her own trace before turning Mara into the next witness.

body.text: Mara steps closer to the empty brass mount and sees a clean soot ring beneath it, the shape of something heated and removed in the last hour. On the side table below, a visitor card tray holds one fresh indentation with no card left inside.
The same burned-film smell threading the hall clings to the tray, as if a projector case was set there while someone stripped a name from the wall.
Too many hands fit the sequence too neatly. Elias could have staged it, a staff hand could have cleared it, or the missing guest could have removed her own trace before Mara ever reached the house.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: You are very good at turning household shame into chronology.

choices.text: Keep the pattern to yourself and leave with the sequence intact.

risks: Mara leaves with a theory, not a confession.

choices.text: Ring for the staff member who cleared the tray and watch who Elias protects first.

risks: Bringing a second witness into the scene multiplies theories and burns social cover.

choices.text: Read the sequence aloud: missing name, removed card, missing projection case.

risks: If the theory is weak, Elias can still reframe the evening as paranoia.

nodes.notes:
# Nameplate Audit

Shared investigation beat that converts atmosphere clues into a causal theory.

nodes.title: Service Landing

thriller.promise: Bringing staff into the scene breaks Elias's monopoly on interpretation.
thriller.mysteryQuestion: Is Sabine protecting Elias, protecting the house, or cleaning up after the missing guest?

theorySeeds.basis: Elias lets staff absorb the risk while he manages the script.
Sabine has both access and motive to strip the gallery and visitor tray.
Clara knew enough about the reel change to weaponize her own disappearance.

body.text: The service bell barely sounds before Sabine Vale appears from the narrow landing beside the gallery, dry-gloved and unstartled, with a linen cloth folded too carefully over one hand.

body.speaker: char.housekeeper
body.text: If Mr. Voss asked you not to touch the tray, he expected to be obeyed.
When Sabine shifts the cloth, Mara catches a damp card corner and a note in another hand. The line is brief and ugly: If he changes the reel, do not stay for the second performance.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: Sabine clears what the weather and the family leave exposed. That is not the same thing as authorship.

choices.text: Corner Sabine on access, timing, and the missing plate before Elias can answer for her.

risks: Sabine may become the cleanest available culprit whether or not she is the architect.

choices.text: Treat the warning note as a deliberate move by the missing guest, not a piece of housekeeping.

risks: The theory turns on an absent actor who cannot be pressured in the room.

nodes.notes:
# Service Landing

Social pressure node that widens the suspect field past Elias alone.

thriller.endingContract: Curiosity over caution exposes the room Elias was trying to keep outside the night's script.

body.text: The door gives way on the second turn. Behind it, an old projector throws Mara's own published byline across the wall in trembling light.

body.speaker: char.host
body.text: You found the version of the story I prepared for the guest I expected, not the one who actually came.
